label: Seoul
sub: Capital of South Korea — established as Joseon capital 1394; population 10M

label: Panmunjom / JSA
sub: Korean Armistice signed July 27, 1953; Joint Security Area, only point of DPRK-ROK contact

label: Gyeongju
sub: Capital of Silla Kingdom (57 BCE–935 CE); UNESCO World Heritage historic area

label: Gaeseong (Kaesong)
sub: Capital of Goryeo dynasty (918–1392 CE); now in North Korea; former Kaesong Industrial Complex

label: Pyongyang
sub: DPRK capital; ancient Goguryeo stronghold; Lelang Han commandery site; 1950 Korean War focal point

label: Inchon
sub: Operation Chromite amphibious landing September 15, 1950; MacArthur's decisive maneuver

label: Busan
sub: Last UN/ROK defensive perimeter August 1950; wartime capital; major port city

label: Gwangju
sub: May 18–27, 1980 pro-democracy uprising violently suppressed by Chun Doo-hwan's military

label: Dokdo (Liancourt Rocks)
sub: Disputed island: South Korea administers, Japan claims as Takeshima; Korean Coast Guard stationed

label: Haeinsa Temple
sub: Houses Tripitaka Koreana (80,000+ woodblocks, 1251 CE); UNESCO World Heritage Site

label: Gyeongbokgung Palace
sub: Primary Joseon palace built 1395; site of Empress Myeongseong murder 1895; restored post-Japanese demolition

label: Tapgol Park
sub: Declaration of Korean Independence read March 1, 1919; starting point of Samil Movement protests

label: Seoul Olympic Stadium
sub: 1988 Summer Olympics opening/closing ceremonies; Korea's global cultural debut

label: Former USFK HQ (Yongsan)
sub: US Forces Korea headquarters 1945–2018; relocated to Camp Humphreys, Pyeongtaek in 2018

label: Camp Humphreys
sub: Current USFK headquarters; largest US overseas military base; 28,500 US troops stationed

label: DMZ (38th Parallel)
sub: 4km-wide Demilitarized Zone; most heavily fortified border in the world; established 1953

label: Jeonju
sub: Birthplace of Joseon royal family; center of traditional Korean culture, bibimbap, and Jeonju Hanok Village

label: Ulsan
sub: World's largest shipbuilding complex (Hyundai Heavy Industries); major petrochemical and auto production center

label: Daejeon
sub: Daedeok Science Town; South Korea's R&D and high-technology research hub; KAIST campus

label: Pyeongchang
sub: 2018 Winter Olympics; inter-Korean diplomacy; joint Korean delegation at opening ceremony

label: Wonsan
sub: North Korean port city; DPRK missile test launch site; 1968 USS Pueblo captured nearby

label: Yeonpyeong Island
sub: North Korea shelled the island November 23, 2010 — 170+ artillery rounds, 4 killed; first DPRK bombardment of civilian ROK territory since 1953 armistice

label: Jeju Island
sub: UNESCO World Heritage volcanic island; April 3, 1948 massacre (3rd Parallel Incident) — 14,000-30,000 killed by ROK military

label: Suwon — Hwaseong Fortress
sub: Joseon-era fortress (1794-1796) built by King Jeongjo; UNESCO World Heritage Site; exemplary Joseon military architecture

label: Pohang
sub: POSCO steel complex (est. 1970); first integrated steelworks of Korea's industrial buildup; anchor of Miracle on the Han River
